verses.text: David consults all Israel about bringing back the ark, neglected in Saul's days: 'let us bring again the ark of our God.' His first care as king is the presence of God. A heart after God's own longs to set His worship at the center.

David consults all Israel about bringing back the ark, neglected in Saul's days: 'let us bring again the ark of our God.' His first care as king is the presence of God. A heart after God's own longs to set His worship at the center.

All Israel gathers, and the ark is carried on a new cart amid music and great rejoicing. The joy is real, but the method is wrong — the Philistines' cart, not the Levites' shoulders God commanded. Zeal without obedience courts disaster.

All Israel gathers, and the ark is carried on a new cart amid music and great rejoicing. The joy is real, but the method is wrong — the Philistines' cart, not the Levites' shoulders God commanded. Zeal without obedience courts disaster.

When the oxen stumble and Uzza steadies the ark, God strikes him dead, and David, displeased and afraid, names the place Perez-uzza. The holy God will not be handled carelessly. Good intentions do not excuse disregard of His commands.

When the oxen stumble and Uzza steadies the ark, God strikes him dead, and David, displeased and afraid, names the place Perez-uzza. The holy God will not be handled carelessly. Good intentions do not excuse disregard of His commands.

When the oxen stumble and Uzza steadies the ark, God strikes him dead, and David, displeased and afraid, names the place Perez-uzza. The holy God will not be handled carelessly. Good intentions do not excuse disregard of His commands.

Afraid, David leaves the ark at Obed-edom's house — and God blesses that household richly. The presence that struck Uzza becomes a blessing to the reverent. God is a terror to the careless, a joy to those who welcome Him rightly.
